canonical_runtime.source_archive_evidence_rule: Working trees may retain generated `artifacts/runtime-cert/` bundles for archaeology, but built wheels and source distributions do not ship those directories. No source or built snapshot proves a current PASS unless it is accompanied by an addressable workflow artifact, release evidence bundle, or pinned manifest digest.
canonical_runtime.benchmark_publication_rule: Primary docs may publish benchmark numbers only when each published number maps to an addressable evidence bundle or manifest digest plus exact commit, model ids, MLX version, hardware, script, and invocation arguments.

families.name: llama
families.workflow_story: The release workflow is designed to produce addressable Apple-arm64 certification artifacts for Llama.
families.notes: Source archives alone do not prove a current PASS for Llama; use an addressable evidence bundle or pinned manifest digest.

families.name: gemma
families.workflow_story: The release workflow is designed to produce addressable Apple-arm64 certification artifacts for Gemma.
families.notes: Gemma coverage remains narrower overall because the conservative paper_mse batch quality guardrail remains Llama-scoped, even though PolarQuant runtime and quality evidence now exist for Gemma; source archives alone do not prove a current PASS.

presets.name: paper_prod
presets.notes: Paper-facing production-style preset using a 1-bit QJL residual.

presets.name: high_compression
presets.notes: Legacy convenience alias for the QJL production-style preset.

presets.name: balanced
presets.notes: Legacy top-k compatibility preset; not part of the paper-facing contract.

presets.name: max_quality
presets.notes: Legacy top-k compatibility preset; not part of the paper-facing contract.

presets.name: polarquant_exp
presets.notes: Supported non-paper-facing PolarQuant branch with family-scoped runtime and quality certification.

secondary_surfaces.gate: constructs TurboQuantKCache directly for comparison harnesses

deviations.title: Non-power-of-two Hadamard handling
deviations.description: The implementation uses an exact Hadamard transform only for power-of-two head dimensions and a deterministic orthogonal fallback otherwise.

deviations.title: Legacy compatibility knobs
deviations.description: Legacy aliases, residual_topk, and block_tokens remain for compatibility, but they are not part of the paper-facing preset contract.

deviations.title: Vendored tree wider than support boundary
deviations.description: The vendored mlx_lm tree contains many model files, but only the allowlisted families in this contract are supported by the canonical upgrade path.

deviations.title: Compatibility and non-paper-facing branches
deviations.description: legacy_topk remains a compatibility branch, while polarquant_exp is now a supported non-paper-facing branch: PolarQuant works through the allowlisted upgrade_cache_list path, has Llama and Gemma certification runtime smoke stages, and family-scoped batch quality guardrails, but it remains outside the paper-facing preset story.
